evals.name: courses-mn-bridge
evals.prompt: I'm building a small online course platform. Students can enroll in many courses, each course has one instructor, and I need to track each enrollment's date and whether the student completed the course. Design the data model for me.
evals.expected_output: A normalized DBML model with a students↔courses M:N resolved via an enrollments bridge table (carrying enrolled_at + a completion status), TypeID text PKs on every table, instructor as a 1:N FK on courses, plus a validation report and a green PGlite live-test.

assertions.text: A .dbml model file is produced as the deliverable
The user stated no PK preference, so every table's primary key is a text `id [pk]` TypeID (the recommended default)
Each table records its TypeID prefix in a Note
The students-courses M:N is resolved with a bridge/junction table (not a direct many-to-many)
The bridge table carries the relationship attributes (enrollment date and completion status)
Foreign key columns are typed text and use inline [ref: > ...]
The PGlite live-test harness (run-erd-test.mjs) was actually run and every use-case passed (green)
A validation/best-practices report was produced

evals.name: saas-billing-enums
evals.prompt: Design a database schema for a B2B SaaS subscription billing system. Organizations each have multiple users. An organization holds subscriptions tied to a plan, and each subscription generates invoices. Subscriptions can be active, past_due, or canceled; invoices are open, paid, or void. Model this domain.
evals.expected_output: A normalized DBML model with org-scoped multi-tenancy (org FK right after the PK), Enums for subscription_status (active/past_due/canceled) and invoice_status (open/paid/void), TypeID text PKs everywhere, plus a validation report and a green PGlite live-test.

assertions.text: A .dbml model file is produced as the deliverable
The user stated no PK preference, so every table's primary key is a text `id [pk]` TypeID (the recommended default)
Status fields use DBML Enums rather than free-text or boolean-soup columns
The subscription status enum has exactly the values active, past_due, canceled (spelling preserved)
The invoice status enum has exactly the values open, paid, void (spelling preserved)
Multi-tenant child tables carry an organization FK placed immediately after the PK
The PGlite live-test harness (run-erd-test.mjs) was actually run and every use-case passed (green)
A validation/best-practices report was produced

evals.name: projects-self-ref
evals.prompt: Create an ERD for a project management tool. Projects contain tasks, a task can have subtasks (nested arbitrarily deep), each task is assigned to one user, and users belong to teams. I want to be able to delete a project and have its tasks go away, but deleting a user should just unassign their tasks.
evals.expected_output: A normalized DBML model with a self-referential nullable parent_task_id on tasks, ON DELETE CASCADE from project→task and ON DELETE SET NULL on task.assigned_user_id, TypeID text PKs everywhere, plus a validation report and a green PGlite live-test that proves both delete behaviors.

assertions.text: A .dbml model file is produced as the deliverable
The user stated no PK preference, so every table's primary key is a text `id [pk]` TypeID (the recommended default)
The tasks table has a self-referential parent FK that is nullable (so root tasks have no parent)
The project→task FK carries ON DELETE CASCADE and the task→user assignment FK carries ON DELETE SET NULL (recorded as // comments)
The live-test includes use-cases proving both ON DELETE behaviors (cascade delete and set-null)
The PGlite live-test harness (run-erd-test.mjs) was actually run and every use-case passed (green)
A validation/best-practices report was produced

evals.name: uuid-pk-preference
evals.prompt: Model a simple blog: authors write posts, posts have comments. Use UUID primary keys — our stack already uses uuids everywhere.
evals.expected_output: A normalized DBML model honoring the stated UUID identity strategy: every PK is `id uuid [pk, default: \`gen_random_uuid()\`]` and every FK is typed uuid, no TypeID prefixes, plus a validation report and a green PGlite live-test (which supplies explicit uuid literals for asserted rows).

assertions.text: A .dbml model file is produced as the deliverable
The user asked for UUIDs, so every table's PK is `id uuid [pk]` with a gen_random_uuid() default (NOT a TypeID text id, NOT int/serial)
Foreign key columns are typed uuid to match the referenced uuid PK
No TypeID prefix Notes are added (the prefix Note is a TypeID-only requirement)
The PGlite live-test harness (run-erd-test.mjs) was actually run and every use-case passed (green), with explicit uuid literals used for any asserted ids
A validation/best-practices report was produced

evals.name: int-pk-preference
evals.prompt: Design a schema for a small internal library catalog: books, members, and loans (a member borrows a book). Keep it simple — just use auto-incrementing integer IDs.
evals.expected_output: A normalized DBML model honoring the stated auto-increment identity strategy: every PK is `id bigint [pk, increment]` (mapped to GENERATED BY DEFAULT AS IDENTITY / bigserial in the DDL) and every FK is typed bigint, loans resolving the member↔book M:N as a bridge, plus a validation report and a green PGlite live-test that supplies explicit small-integer ids.

assertions.text: A .dbml model file is produced as the deliverable
The user asked for auto-increment integers, so every table's PK is an integer identity `id bigint [pk, increment]` (NOT a TypeID text id, NOT uuid)
Foreign key columns are typed bigint to match the referenced integer PK
The member-borrows-book relationship is resolved with a loans bridge/junction table
The PGlite live-test harness (run-erd-test.mjs) was actually run and every use-case passed (green), with explicit integer ids used in seed/use-case rows
A validation/best-practices report was produced

evals.name: spec-pipeline-usecase-intake
evals.prompt: Build the data model for this project from its spec (the spec/ directory contains glossary.md with terms mapped to ERD tables and an enumerations table, and usecases.md with UC-001..UC-003 carrying data assertions like `=> expect: rowcount=1` and `=> expect: error ~ foreign key`).
evals.expected_output: A DBML model derived from the glossary (tables and enums verbatim), live-tested with one block per use-case data assertion labeled `-- usecase: UC-xxx/DA-n …`, every assertion green, the model saved to spec/data/model.dbml and the final usecases.sql persisted to spec/data/usecases.sql.

assertions.text: Tables and enums come verbatim from the glossary (Maps to: ERD: names, exact enum value spellings)
Every active use case's data assertions appear as live-test blocks labeled `-- usecase: UC-xxx/DA-n <description>` with the DA's assertion as the -- expect:
The PGlite live-test harness (run-erd-test.mjs) was actually run and every use-case passed (green)
The model is saved to spec/data/model.dbml
The final usecases.sql is persisted to spec/data/usecases.sql (the traceability record the ddd-align gate audits)
A validation/best-practices report was produced
